proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope of pfs_months on age_years).

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.linregress(df['age_years'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Linear regression of pfs_months on age_years: slope = +0.176 months/year (p < 1e-300). Direction is OPPOSITE of the hypothesis: in this cohort, older patients have LONGER PFS, not shorter.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs between female (sex_female=1) and male (sex_female=0) patients.
Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative trend).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: female 4.686 vs male 4.699 months; t=-0.58, p=0.56. No detectable difference by sex.

analyses.code: stats.linregress(df['ecog_ps'], df['pfs_months']); also one-way ANOVA across ECOG 0/1/2
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by ECOG: 0=5.64, 1=4.44, 2=3.29 months. Linear trend slope = -1.180 months per ECOG unit (p<1e-300). Strongly supports the hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage-IV disease.
Presence of brain metastases (has_brain_mets=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Node-positive disease (node_positive=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than node-negative disease.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: stage IV 3.62 vs non-IV 5.15 months; diff = -1.54 months, t=-65.7, p<1e-300. Strongly supports.
Mean PFS: brain mets 3.75 vs none 4.80 months; diff = -1.05 months, p=1.8e-179. Strongly supports.
Mean PFS: node+ 4.681 vs node- 4.705 months; diff = -0.024 months, p=0.29. Not significant; node status alone has no detectable effect on PFS in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Postmenopausal patients (postmenopausal=1) have different mean pfs_months than premenopausal patients.
ER-positive patients (er_positive=1) have longer pfs_months than ER-negative patients.
PR-positive patients (pr_positive=1) have longer pfs_months than PR-negative patients.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: postmenopausal 4.684 vs premenopausal 4.707 months; diff=-0.023, p=0.31. Not significant.
Mean PFS: ER+ 4.86 vs ER- 4.30 months; diff = +0.57 months, t=23.5, p=2.2e-121. ER positivity confers longer PFS, supports hypothesis.
Mean PFS: PR+ 4.82 vs PR- 4.46 months; diff = +0.36, p=8e-55. PR positivity confers longer PFS, supports hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: HER2-positive patients (her2_positive=1) have shorter pfs_months than HER2-negative patients.
HER2-low patients (her2_low=1) have different pfs_months than HER2-not-low patients.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: HER2+ 4.32 vs HER2- 4.78 months; diff = -0.46, p=1.2e-56. Strongly supports HER2+ is adverse for PFS in this cohort overall.
Mean PFS: HER2-low 4.76 vs not-low 4.65 months; diff = +0.11, p=7e-7. Small but statistically significant favorable association of HER2-low.

proposed_hypotheses.text: BRCA1-mutation carriers (brca1_mutation=1) have shorter pfs_months than non-carriers.
BRCA2-mutation carriers (brca2_mutation=1) have shorter pfs_months than non-carriers.
PIK3CA-mutation carriers (pik3ca_mutation=1) have shorter pfs_months than non-carriers.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: BRCA1+ 4.67 vs BRCA1- 4.69; diff=-0.024, p=0.73. Not significant.
Mean PFS: BRCA2+ 4.57 vs BRCA2- 4.70; diff=-0.13, p=0.078. Trend, not significant.
Mean PFS: PIK3CA-mut 4.33 vs WT 4.89 months; diff = -0.56, p=2e-126. Strongly supports — PIK3CA mutation is adverse for PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ki67_pct (proliferation index) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Larger tumor_size_cm is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Slope = -0.0188 months per % Ki67 (r=-0.091, p=3e-93). Supports: higher Ki67 → shorter PFS.
Slope = +0.00055 months/cm; r≈0.0004, p=0.93. No association of tumor size with PFS in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Slope = +0.499 months per g/dL albumin, r=+0.100, p=9e-112. Strongly supports.
Slope = -0.000507 months per U/L LDH (r=-0.016, p=2.4e-4). Statistically significant but very small magnitude.
Slope = -0.0747 months per % weight loss, r=-0.116, p=4e-148. Strongly supports.
Slope = -0.00088 months per mg/L; r=-0.003, p=0.46. Not significant.
Slope = +0.0033 months per NLR unit; r=0.003, p=0.54. Not significant — direction opposite to expected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl, no clinically meaningful linear associations with pfs_months will be detected.

analyses.result_summary: Slopes (per natural unit) and p-values: hemoglobin -0.006 (p=0.30), alk_phos -0.00067 (p=0.003), AST -0.00056 (p=0.56), ALT -0.00073 (p=0.42), bilirubin -0.07 (p=0.046), creatinine -0.033 (p=0.38), BUN -0.00057 (p=0.80), Na -0.0016 (p=0.67), K +0.050 (p=0.025), Ca -0.064 (p=0.004). All effects are tiny in clinical magnitude, even when p<0.05; no lab in this group is a strong PFS driver.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen=1 have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (in the marginal, unadjusted comparison).
Patients receiving treatment_palbociclib=1 have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (marginal).
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab=1 have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (marginal).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: tam 4.72 vs no-tam 4.68; diff = +0.043, p=0.077. Not significant. Tamoxifen has no detectable marginal benefit.
Mean PFS: palbo 5.41 vs no-palbo 4.31; diff = +1.10 months, t=47.9, p<1e-300. Marginal benefit highly significant.
Mean PFS: tras 4.64 vs no-tras 4.71; diff = -0.060, p=0.030. Slight negative marginal association — likely confounding by HER2 status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib=1 have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (marginal).
Patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan=1 have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (marginal).
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab=1 have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (marginal).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: olap 4.66 vs none 4.70; diff = -0.036, p=0.33. No marginal benefit.
Mean PFS: saci 4.69 vs none 4.69; diff = -0.007, p=0.84. No marginal benefit.
Mean PFS: pembro 4.63 vs none 4.70; diff = -0.074, p=0.017. Slight negative marginal association — likely confounding by indication (TNBC).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for demographics, performance status, stage, brain mets, ER/PR/HER2/HER2-low, BRCA1/BRCA2/PIK3CA, Ki67, tumor size, albumin, LDH, weight loss, CRP, NLR, hemoglobin, and alkaline phosphatase, only treatment_palbociclib retains a clinically meaningful adjusted PFS benefit; the other five treatments do not.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months on covariates + 6 treatment indicators
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment betas (months): tamoxifen -0.008 (p=0.49), palbociclib +1.10 (p<1e-300), trastuzumab -0.009 (p=0.46), olaparib -0.026 (p=0.12), sacituzumab +0.033 (p=0.049), pembrolizumab -0.019 (p=0.17). Strongly supports: palbociclib is the only treatment with a substantial average adjusted PFS benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Tamoxifen × er_positive interaction: tamoxifen prolongs pfs_months more in ER-positive than ER-negative patients (positive interaction term on PFS).

analyses.result_summary: Tx effect in ER- = +0.081 (p=0.065); tx effect in ER+ = +0.023; interaction term = -0.058 (p=0.27). Not significant — tamoxifen has no biomarker-defined PFS benefit, even in the canonical ER+ indication.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Palbociclib × er_positive interaction: palbociclib prolongs pfs_months more in ER-positive than ER-negative patients (positive interaction term).
Palbociclib × her2_positive interaction: palbociclib effect on pfs_months is larger in HER2-negative than HER2-positive patients (negative interaction term with HER2 positivity).

analyses.result_summary: Tx effect in ER- = -0.017; tx effect in ER+ = +1.577; interaction = +1.594 (p=9.3e-229). Strongly supports: palbociclib benefit is concentrated in ER-positive patients.
Tx effect in HER2+ = -0.014; tx effect in HER2- = +1.350; interaction with HER2-negative = +1.364 (p=2.2e-118). Strongly supports: palbociclib benefit is concentrated in HER2-negative patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Palbociclib × (er_positive AND her2_negative) joint subgroup: palbociclib prolongs pfs_months substantially in patients who are simultaneously ER-positive and HER2-negative, but not otherwise.

analyses.code: Define mod = (er_positive==1)&(her2_positive==0); OLS pfs_months ~ tx + mod + tx*mod
analyses.result_summary: Tx effect in non-(ER+/HER2-) = -0.006 (≈0); tx effect in ER+/HER2- = +1.924 months; interaction = +1.930 (p<1e-300). Strongly supports the joint definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Trastuzumab × her2_positive interaction: trastuzumab prolongs pfs_months more in HER2-positive than HER2-negative patients (positive interaction term).
Sacituzumab govitecan × TNBC (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0): sacituzumab prolongs pfs_months more in TNBC than non-TNBC patients (positive interaction term).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ tx + her2_positive + tx*her2_positive; also subgroup ttest in HER2+
analyses.result_summary: Interaction term = -0.028 (p=0.69); not significant. Subgroup ttest within HER2+ (N=9060, tras N=1890): mean PFS 4.25 vs 4.33, diff = -0.079, p=0.15. Refutes hypothesis — no PFS benefit of trastuzumab in HER2+ in this dataset.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ tx + tnbc + tx*tnbc; also subgroup ttest in TNBC
analyses.result_summary: Interaction term = -0.105 (p=0.25); not significant. Subgroup ttest in TNBC (N=10496, saci N=1084): diff = -0.085, p=0.21. Refutes hypothesis — no PFS benefit of sacituzumab in TNBC in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Olaparib × any-BRCA-mutation (brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1): olaparib prolongs pfs_months more in BRCA-mutated than BRCA-WT patients (positive interaction term).
Pembrolizumab × TNBC (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0): pembrolizumab prolongs pfs_months more in TNBC than non-TNBC patients (positive interaction term).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ tx + brca_any + tx*brca_any; subgroup ttest in BRCA+
analyses.result_summary: Interaction term = +0.402 (p=0.021); marginally significant. Within BRCA+ (N=2499, olap N=239): mean PFS olap 4.93 vs 4.59, diff = +0.346, p=0.040. Supports a small olaparib benefit confined to BRCA-mutated patients.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ tx + tnbc + tx*tnbc; subgroup ttest in TNBC
analyses.result_summary: Interaction = +0.185 (p=0.015); significant. Effect in non-TNBC = -0.114; effect in TNBC = +0.072 (subgroup ttest p=0.21). Supports a small benefit of pembrolizumab confined to TNBC.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ER-positive HER2-negative patients, palbociclib × pik3ca_mutation interaction is negative: palbociclib prolongs pfs_months in PIK3CA wild-type but not in PIK3CA-mutated patients.
Within ER-positive HER2-negative patients, palbociclib effect on pfs_months declines with rising ki67_pct (negative tx*ki67 interaction).

analyses.code: Within ER+/HER2- (N=28589): OLS pfs_months ~ tx + pik3ca + tx*pik3ca
analyses.result_summary: Interaction = -2.886 (p<1e-300). Tx effect in PIK3CA-WT = +2.908 months; in PIK3CA-mut = +0.022 (essentially zero). Strongly supports: PIK3CA mutation abolishes the palbociclib PFS benefit.

analyses.code: Within ER+/HER2- (N=28589): OLS pfs_months ~ tx + ki67_pct + tx*ki67_pct
analyses.result_summary: tx*ki67 interaction = -0.0876 (p=8e-266). Tx effect declines linearly with Ki67. Strongly supports the hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Palbociclib prolongs pfs_months substantially (~+4 months) and exclusively in patients who are simultaneously er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct < 20; outside this subgroup palbociclib provides no PFS benefit.

analyses.code: Define G = (er_positive==1)&(her2_positive==0)&(pik3ca_mutation==0)&(ki67_pct<20); ttest(pfs by tx | G), ttest(pfs by tx | not G)
analyses.result_summary: In G (N=14,174, palbo N=4,984): mean PFS palbo 6.66 vs no-palbo 2.81, diff = +3.85 months, p<1e-300. Outside G (N=35,826, palbo N=12,444): diff = -0.002, p=0.94. Adjusted effect inside G (controlling for age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, etc.) = +3.85 (p<1e-300). Strongly supports the four-component subgroup definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT, the palbociclib PFS benefit is large and approximately constant for ki67_pct < ~14 and falls toward zero as Ki67 approaches ~20%, consistent with a continuous Ki67 modifier rather than a sharp threshold.

analyses.code: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT (N=18681): subgroup palbo effect by Ki67 cutpoints, plus continuous interaction model
analyses.result_summary: Palbo effect by Ki67 cutoff in core subgroup: <10: +4.96; <12: +4.96; <14: +4.94; <16: +4.45; <18: +4.10; <20: +3.85; <25: +3.40. Above same cutoffs effect ≈0. Continuous model: palbo_effect ≈ 5.01 - 0.135*Ki67 (interaction p<1e-300), crossing zero near Ki67 = 37%. Supports continuous decline; pragmatic cutoff Ki67<20 captures most of the benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outside palbociclib, no other treatment shows a reproducible biomarker-defined subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>=0.5 month) treatment-effect on pfs_months in this dataset.

analyses.code: For each remaining treatment, regress pfs_months ~ tx + feature + tx*feature for all 31 features and rank by p_interaction
analyses.result_summary: Smallest interaction p-values per treatment (signed effect in months): tamoxifen × stage_iv -0.118 (p=0.021); trastuzumab × tumor_size +0.037 (p=0.018); olaparib × creatinine +0.358 (p=0.005) and × any-BRCA +0.402 (p=0.021); sacituzumab × albumin +0.185 (p=0.013); pembrolizumab × er_positive -0.131 (p=0.050) and × tnbc +0.185 (p=0.015). Aside from olaparib×BRCA and pembrolizumab×TNBC (already pre-specified), no signal exceeds an unadjusted ~0.4-month effect or survives multiple testing for 6 treatments × 31 features. Supports the refined hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within BRCA-mutated patients, olaparib × node_positive interaction is positive: olaparib prolongs pfs_months more in node-positive BRCA-mutated patients than in node-negative BRCA-mutated patients.

The olaparib pfs_months benefit is concentrated in BRCA-mutated patients (no further single binary modifier robustly refines this beyond BRCA status given limited N=239 olaparib-treated BRCA carriers).

analyses.code: Within BRCA+ (N=2499): OLS pfs ~ olap + node_positive + olap*node_positive
analyses.result_summary: Interaction = +0.816 (p=0.016); effect in node- = -0.082 m; effect in node+ = +0.734 m. Statistically significant but based on only 239 olaparib-treated BRCA+ patients; deserves replication, not over-interpretation.

analyses.code: Subgroup tests within BRCA+ for stage_iv, brain mets, ER, HER2, postmenopausal, sex_female, node_positive
analyses.result_summary: Most modifier interactions within BRCA+ are non-significant (p>0.08). Only sex_female (p=0.020) and node_positive (p=0.016) reach p<0.05 in 7 tests; given multiple comparisons and small N, BRCA mutation alone is the most reliable single subgroup definition for olaparib (effect ≈ +0.35 m, p=0.040).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within TNBC (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0), pembrolizumab × postmenopausal interaction is negative: pembrolizumab prolongs pfs_months in premenopausal TNBC but not in postmenopausal TNBC.

analyses.code: Within TNBC (N=10496): OLS pfs ~ pembro + postmeno + pembro*postmeno
analyses.result_summary: Interaction = -0.334 (p=0.005); effect in premeno TNBC = +0.275 (subgroup ttest p=0.003, N=4163, pembro N=611); effect in postmeno TNBC = -0.059 (ns). Supports refined hypothesis: pembrolizumab benefit on PFS in this dataset appears confined to premenopausal TNBC.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL palbociclib subgroup: treatment_palbociclib=1 prolongs pfs_months by approximately +3.8 to +4.7 months (depending on the Ki67 cutoff used) in patients defined by er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct < 20%; effect is null outside this complete four-variable subgroup. Each of the four predicates is required: ER negativity, HER2 positivity, PIK3CA mutation, and high Ki67 each independently abolishes the effect.

analyses.code: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT/Ki67<20 vs complement; sensitivity at Ki67 cutoffs 14/16/20/25; effect estimates within each single-violation subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (N=14,174): palbo effect = +3.85 m (p<1e-300, adjusted +3.85, p<1e-300). Outside subgroup (N=35,826): effect = -0.002 m (p=0.94). Ki67<14 gives effect +4.94 m, <20 gives +3.85 m, <25 gives +3.40 m. Single-predicate violations: ER- → effect ≈ -0.02 m; HER2+ → -0.05 m; PIK3CA-mut (within ER+/HER2-) → +0.02 m; Ki67≥20 (within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT) → -0.03 m. Complete four-variable subgroup definition is required for the palbociclib benefit; this is the best-supported treatment-effect subgroup in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all six treatments, the rank ordering of subgroup-conditional PFS benefit is: palbociclib (in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT/Ki67<20) ≈ +3.8–4.7 m  >>  olaparib (in any-BRCA-mutated) ≈ +0.35 m ≈ pembrolizumab (in premenopausal TNBC) ≈ +0.28 m  >  tamoxifen ≈ trastuzumab ≈ sacituzumab ≈ 0 (null in any tested subgroup, including their canonical indications).

After multivariable adjustment for biomarkers, ECOG, stage, brain mets, labs, and treatments, age_years remains a positive linear predictor of pfs_months (older → longer PFS) — consistent with what is seen in the unadjusted regression, despite being clinically counter-intuitive in real-world oncology.

analyses.code: compile subgroup-specific palbo, olap, pembro effects vs null effects of tam, tras, saci
analyses.result_summary: Verified: palbo +3.85 m in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT/Ki67<20 (p<1e-300); olap +0.35 m in BRCA+ (p=0.040); pembro +0.28 m in premeno TNBC (p=0.003). Tamoxifen +0.044 m in premeno ER+ (p=0.38); trastuzumab -0.079 m in HER2+ (p=0.15); sacituzumab -0.085 m in TNBC (p=0.21). Supports the rank-ordering hypothesis.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS already fit; check β_age
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted age coefficient = +0.176 months/year (p<1e-300). Same direction and similar magnitude as the unadjusted +0.176 months/year. Supports the refined claim. This is likely a feature of the data-generating process rather than a real clinical phenomenon, and should be flagged in any downstream interpretation.
